title: brya buxifolia
intro: Brya buxifolia, also known as the boxleaf tree or the false boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America, and is often found growing in dry forests and scrublands. The medicinal properties of Brya buxifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
